  5. Slowly stir in heavy cream, and add water, pasta, and 1 tsp of salt to the skillet. Cover skillet with a lid and simmer on low heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring pasta periodically to check for sticking.**
  6. While pasta is cooking, combine cottage cheese, spinach, garlic powder, and ¼ tsp of salt in a bowl.
  7. When pasta is al dente or to desired doneness, top with dollops of the cheese and spinach mixture and top with shredded mozzarella. Cover the skillet with a lid to allow the cheese to melt for 1-2 minutes.
  8. Let cool for 5 minutes, and dig in!
    9. See how we calculate recipe costs here .
Notes
  • You can use equal parts chicken or vegetable broth in place of the vodka. Water with a squeeze of lemon (to add a slight acidity that you’d miss out on by replacing the vodka) will also work.
  • *If the sauce becomes too thick and the pasta sticks while cooking, add about a 1/2 cup of water.
Creamy Tomato and Spinach Pasta
Adapted from https://www.budgetbytes.com/creamy-tomato-spinach-pasta/
Tags: budget friendly dinner
Servings: 4
Ingredients
  • ½ lb penne pasta ($0.49)
  • 1 yellow onion (small dice, 340g, 1.5 cups, $0.78)
  • 2 cloves garlic (minced, 1 Tbsp, $0.12)
  • 1 Tbsp olive oil ($0.18)
  • 14.5 oz. diced tomatoes (1 can, $0.96)
  • ½ tsp dried oregano ($0.12)
  • ½ tsp dried basil ($0.13)
  • ⅛ tsp crushed red pepper ($0.02)
  • ½ tsp salt ($0.02)
  • ½ tsp freshly cracked black pepper ($0.08)
  • 2 Tbsp tomato paste ($0.14)
  • ½ cup water ($0.00)
  • 2 oz cream cheese ($0.46)
  • ¼ cup grated Parmesan (30g, $0.51)
  • 4 oz fresh spinach ($0.79)
Steps
  1. Gather and prepare all ingredients.
  2. Bring a large pot of water to a boil over high heat. Add the pasta and continue to boil until tender (7-10 minutes). Drain the pasta in a colander.
  3. While the pasta is cooking, prepare the creamy tomato sauce. Dice the onion and mince the garlic. Add the onion, garlic, and olive oil to a large skillet and sauté over medium heat until the onions are soft and translucent (3-5 minutes).
  4. Add the diced tomatoes (with juices), oregano, basil, crushed red pepper, salt, and some freshly cracked pepper to the skillet. Stir to combine. Add the tomato paste and a 1/2 cup of water to the skillet and stir until the tomato paste has mixed into the sauce.
  5. Turn the heat down to low. Cut the cream cheese into a few pieces and then add them to the skillet with the tomato sauce. Use a whisk to stir the sauce until the cream cheese has fully melted and the sauce is creamy. Add the Parmesan to the skillet and stir until it is melted into the sauce.
  6. Add the fresh spinach to the skillet and gently stir it into the sauce until it has wilted (2-3 minutes). Add the pasta and stir until it is well coated in the creamy tomato sauce. Taste and adjust the salt and pepper as needed. Serve warm.
3 Ingredient Frozen Yogurt
Adapted from https://www.budgetbytes.com/3-ingredient-frozen-yogurt/
Tags: budget friendly dessert
Ingredients
  • 1 14 oz. can sweetened condensed milk ($1.59)
  • 1 quart full fat Greek yogurt ($4.12)
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract ($0.50)
Steps
  1. Gather the ingredients.
  2. Combine all the ingredients in a large mixing bowl and mix on high speed with a hand mixer until completely combined, smooth, and fluffy, about 5-8 minutes.
  3. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and transfer to the freezer for 8 hours. Make sure your bowl is freezer-safe!
  4. Scoop and enjoy!
Homemade Mac and Cheese (Easy and Cheesy!)
Adapted from https://www.budgetbytes.com/extra-cheesy-homemade-mac-and-cheese/
Tags: budget friendly dinner
Servings: 4 about 1.25 cups each
Ingredients
  • 8 oz. macaroni ($0.49)
  • 3 Tbsp butter ($0.39)
  • 3 Tbsp all-purpose flour ($0.06)
  • 1/2 tsp onion powder ($0.03)
  • 2 cups whole milk ($0.36)
  • 1/2 tsp hot sauce* ($0.02)
  • 1/2 tsp salt ($0.01)
  • 8 oz. block sharp cheddar, shredded** ($2.24)
Steps
  1. Cook the macaroni according to the package directions (boil for 7-8 minutes, or until tender). Drain the macaroni in a colander.
  2. Place the butter, flour, and onion powder in a medium pot. Whisk and heat over medium until the butter is melted and the mixture begins to bubble. Once bubbling, continue to whisk and cook for one minute.
  3. After cooking the butter and flour roux, whisk in the milk. Continue to cook and whisk over medium heat until the milk begins to gently simmer, at which point it will thicken to the consistency of heavy cream or gravy.
    4. Make sure the milk has come to a simmer and fully thickened before beginning to add the cheese. If the starches in the roux have not plumped and thickened the sauce yet, the cheese will not melt in smoothly.
  4. Turn the burner off. Season the white sauce with the salt and hot sauce.
  5. Begin to add the shredded cheddar, one handful at a time, whisking it in until completely melted before adding more. Continue until all of the cheese has been melted into the sauce. If the sauce becomes too cold to melt the cheese, place it back over low heat only briefly to continue melting the cheese.
    6. Do not let the cheese sauce boil. Overheating the cheese sauce can cause it to separate and become grainy, so after the milk has thickened, turn off the heat before adding the cheese.
  6. Add the cooked and drained pasta to the pot with the cheese sauce and stir to combine. If your pasta appears to be drowning in cheese, don't worry! It'll be the perfect consistency soon as the macaroni absorbs the sauce and thickens up.
  7. Serve hot and enjoy!
    8. BAKED MAC AND CHEESE:
  8. Preheat the oven to 350ºF
  9. After combining the drained pasta and cheese sauce, transfer it to a casserole dish.
  10. Top with more shredded cheese, then bake for 15 minutes.
  11. To achieve a golden brown crispy cheese crust, turn on the oven’s broiler for just a few short minutes, watching it closely to prevent burning.
Best Homemade Meatloaf
Adapted from https://www.budgetbytes.com/classic-homemade-meatloaf/
Tags: budget friendly dinner
Servings: 7 slices
Ingredients
    MEATLOAF:
  • 1 large egg ($0.33)
  • 1/3 cup milk ($0.06)
  • 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce ($0.02)
  • 1/2 cup plain breadcrumbs ($0.32)
  • 1 tsp Italian seasoning ($0.17)
  • 1/2 tsp garlic powder ($0.02)
  • 1 tsp salt ($0.01)
  • 1/4 tsp freshly cracked black pepper ($0.05)
  • 1/2 cup minced onion ($0.22)
  • 1.5 lbs. ground beef (80/20) ($5.91)
    • GLAZE:
  • 1/2 cup ketchup ($0.24)
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar ($0.03)
  • 1 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce ($0.05)
  • 1 tsp yellow mustard ($0.01)
Steps
  1. Preheat the oven to 350ºF. In a small bowl whisk together the egg, milk, and Worcestershire sauce. In a separate small bowl, stir together the breadcrumbs, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Mince the onion.
  2. Add the ground beef, egg mixture, breadcrumb mixture, and minced onion to a large bowl. Use your hands to mix the ingredients together until evenly combined. Avoid over mixing.
  3. Place the meatloaf mixture on a rimmed baking dish and shape it into a loaf that is approximately 4-inches wide, 8-inches long, and 2-inches tall.
  4. In a separate small bowl, stir together the glaze ingredients (ketchup, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, and mustard). Spread the glaze evenly over the top and sides of the meatloaf.
  5. Bake the meatloaf for 50-55 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 165ºF. Allow the meatloaf to rest for 5-10 minutes after baking, then slice and serve.
Cherry Fluff Salad
Adapted from https://www.swankyrecipes.com/cherry-fluff-salad.html?back=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.google.com%2Fsearch%3Fclient%3Dsafari%26as_qdr%3Dall%26as_occt%3Dany%26safe%3Dactive%26as_q%3DCherry+fluffed+salad%26channel%3Daplab%26source%3Da-app1%26hl%3Den
Tags: dessert
Rated 2/5
Servings: 13
Ingredients
  • 21 ounces canned cherry pie filling
  • 8 ounces whipped cream topping
  • 14 ounces sweetened condensed milk
  • ½ tsp almond extract
  • 10 ounces mini marshmallows
  • 20 ounces crushed pineapple
Steps
  1. In a large mixing bowl, mix together whipped topping, cherry pie filling, and sweetened condensed milk.
  2. Stir in almond extract, mini marshmallows, and crushed pineapple in juice.
  3. Cover the bowl and refrigerate until ready to serve.
  4. Cherry Fluff Salad will keep for several days in the refrigerator.
Notes
  • Salad will keep for several days covered in the refrigerator.
  • Make Ahead – Salad can be made in advance. Simply drain the crushed pineapple from the can and discard the liquid. As the cherry dessert salad sits in the refrigerator, the juices will soak into the marshmallows and melt the sugar. This is will add extra liquid to the summer salad.
Creamed Spinach
Adapted from https://www.skinnytaste.com/creamed-spinach-lightened-up/
Tags: healthier sides
Ingredients
  • 2 tsp butter
  • 1/2 cup shallots, minced
  • 1 clove garlic, minced
  • 2 tbsp flour
  • 1 1/2 cups fat free milk
  • 2 tbsp Parmesan cheese
  • 1/4 tsp nutmeg
  • 1/4 tsp fresh black pepper
  • 1/4 tsp kosher salt to taste
  • 4 oz Boursin, garlic and herb (from 1 container)
  • 16 ounce bag frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained of all moisture
Steps
  1. In a large skillet, melt butter over medium-low heat. Add the shallots and garlic and cook until soft, 4 to 5 minutes.
  2. Add the flour to shallots, stir and cook 1 more minute to let the flour cook out.
  3. Reduce heat to low and slowly add milk, whisking well.
  4. Add the parmesan cheese, nutmeg, salt and pepper and mix well.
  5. Add the boursin cheese and mix with until smooth.
  6. Add the thawed spinach and combine well with sauce, cook 1 minute, until heated through. Adjust salt and pepper if necessary.
Basic Sautéed Kale
Adapted from https://www.eatingwell.com/recipe/249029/basic-sauteed-kale/
Tags: healthier sides
Servings: 4 servings, about 1/2 cup each
Ingredients
  • 1 tablespoon plus 1 teaspoon extra-virgin olive oil, divided
  • 1-1 1/2 pounds kale, ribs removed, coarsely chopped (see Tip)
  • ½ cup water
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • ¼ teaspoon crushed red pepper
  • 2-3 teaspoons sherry vinegar, or red-wine vinegar
  • ¼ teaspoon salt
Steps
  1. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a Dutch oven over medium heat. Add kale and cook, tossing with two large spoons, until bright green, about 1 minute. Add water, reduce heat to medium-low, cover and cook, stirring occasionally, until the kale is tender, 12 to 15 minutes. Push kale to one side, add the remaining 1 teaspoon oil to the empty side and cook garlic and crushed red pepper in it until fragrant, 30 seconds to 1 minute. Remove from the heat and toss together. Stir in vinegar to taste and salt.
Notes
  • Tip: A 1- to 1 1/2-pound bunch of kale yields 16 to 24 cups of chopped leaves. When preparing kale for these recipes, remove the tough ribs, chop or tear the kale as directed, then wash it--allowing some water to cling to the leaves. The moisture helps steam the kale during the first stages of cooking.
Stuffed Shells with Meat Sauce
Adapted from https://www.skinnytaste.com/spinach-stuffed-shells-with-meat-sauce/
Tags: dinner healthier
Servings: Serving Size: 3 stuffed shells
Ingredients
  • 27 Jumbo Pasta Shells, such as barilla, or gf pasta, 9 ounces total
  • 1 cup onion, finely chopped
  • 2 garlic cloves, chopped
  • 1 tsp olive oil
  • 1 lb 99% lean ground turkey, or 93% lean
  • 32 oz crushed tomatoes, I used Tuttorosso
  • 1 tbsp chopped fresh basil
  • salt and pepper, to taste
  • 2 cups part-skim ricotta cheese, or cottage cheese for more protein
  • 8 oz part-skim mozzarella cheese, shredded
  • 1 large egg
  • 16 oz package frozen spinach, thawed and squeezed well
  • 1/4 cup Parmigiano Reggiano, or parmesan cheese
Steps
  1. Prep the Pasta Shells: Cook the jumbo pasta shells according to the package instructions until al dente. Drain and set aside.
    2. Prepare the Meat Sauce:
  2. Meanwhile, saute onions and garlic in oil.
  3. Add the ground turkey and salt and brown until cooked, breaking up in small pieces with a wooden spoon.
  4. Stir in the tomatoes, salt, pepper and basil, then simmer on low, covered, about 15 minutes, until the flavors meld.
    5. Prepare the Cheese Filling:
  5. In a large bowl, mix together ricotta, egg, spinach, mozzarella, and parmesan.
    6. Assemble The Stuffed Shells:
  6. Once shells are cooked and cool, fill each shell with (about 2 heaping tbsp) cheese mixture and place on a large baking dish, or two smaller dishes, covering the bottom of the dish with a little sauce.
    7. Bake:
  7. Preheat oven to 375F.
  8. Top shells with half of the sauce, cover with foil and bake 40 minutes; uncover foil and bake 5 more minutes.
  9. Serve with additional sauce on top and garnish with fresh basil, if desired.
Tangy Cream Cheese Lemon Cookies with a Soft, Chewy Texture and Zesty Lemon Glaze
Adapted from https://yummyolk.com/tangy-cream-cheese-lemon-cookies-with-a-soft-chewy-texture-and-zesty-lemon-glaze/
Tags: dessert
Servings: 24 cookies
Ingredients
    For the Cookies:
  • ½ cup unsalted butter, softened
  • ¼ cup cream cheese, softened
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • ½ teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 2– 3 tablespoons lemon zest (from about 2 large lemons)
  • 3 tablespoons fresh lemon juice
  • 2 ½ to 2 ¾ cups all-purpose flour (add more if the dough is too soft)
  • 2 teaspoons cornstarch
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • ¼ teaspoon salt
    • For the Lemon Icing:
  • 1 ½ tablespoons lemon juice (or water)
  • Leftover lemon zest (for garnish)
  • ¾ to 1 cup powdered sugar
Steps
  1. Prepare the Dough
    2. In a large mixing bowl, cream together the softened butter, cream cheese, and granulated sugar using an electric mixer. Beat on medium speed until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).
    Add the egg, vanilla extract, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Continue mixing until well combined.
    In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, baking soda, and salt.
    Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, mixing until just combined. If the dough is too soft, add an additional ¼ cup of flour.
  2. Chill the Dough
    3. Cover the dough with plastic wrap and refrigerate it for at least 30 minutes.
    Chilling the dough is essential to prevent the cookies from spreading too much while baking.
  3. Preheat the Oven
    4. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).
    Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat for easy cleanup.
  4. Shape the Cookies
    5. Scoop about 1 ½ tablespoons of dough and roll it into a ball.
    Place the dough balls on the prepared baking sheet, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
  5. Bake
    6. Bake the cookies for 10–12 minutes, or until the edges are lightly golden and the centers look set.
    Avoid overbaking to keep the cookies soft and chewy.
    Let the cookies rest on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring them to a wire rack to cool completely.
  6. Make the Lemon Icing
    7. In a small bowl, whisk together the lemon juice (or water) and powdered sugar until smooth and pourable.
    Add extra powdered sugar for a thicker glaze or a few more drops of lemon juice for a thinner consistency.
  7. Ice the Cookies
    8. Once the cookies are completely cooled, drizzle the lemon icing over them using a spoon or piping bag.
    Garnish with leftover lemon zest for a bright and decorative touch.
Notes
  • Storage: Store the cookies in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 3 days or in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
  • Freezing: Freeze the unbaked dough balls for up to 3 months. Bake directly from frozen, adding 1–2 minutes to the bake time.
  • Customization: Add poppy seeds, white chocolate chips, or shredded coconut to the dough for extra texture and flavor.
  • Prep Time: 20 minutes
  • Cook Time: 45 minutes
  • Category: Dessert
  • Method: Baking
Lemon Sugar Cookies
Adapted from https://deliceflash.com/lemon-sugar-cookies/
Tags: dessert
I made this.
Ingredients
  • All-Purpose Flour: 2 ½ cups
  • Baking Soda: 1 teaspoon
  • Salt: ½ teaspoon
  • Unsalted Butter: ½ cup (softened)
  • Granulated Sugar: 1 ½ cups (plus extra for rolling)
  • Lemon Zest: 2 tablespoons
  • Lemon Juice: 2 tablespoons (freshly squeezed)
  • Egg: 1 large
  • Vanilla Extract: 1 teaspoon
Steps
  1. Preheat Your Equipment: Preheat oven to 350°F and line baking sheets with parchment.
  2. Mix Dry Ingredients: Whisk flour, baking soda, and salt together.
  3. Cream Butter & Sugar: Beat butter, sugar, and lemon zest until light and fluffy. Add egg, lemon juice, and vanilla; mix well.
  4. Combine Wet & Dry: Add dry mixture gradually to wet, mixing until just combined.
  5. Shape the Cookies: Scoop tablespoon-sized dough balls, roll in sugar, and place 2 inches apart.
  6. Cook to Perfection: Bake 9–11 minutes, until edges are set and centers look slightly soft.
  7. Finishing Touches: Cool 5 minutes on baking sheet, then transfer to rack.
  8. Serve and Enjoy: Enjoy warm or store for later.
Notes
  • Rub lemon zest into sugar before mixing for stronger flavor.
  • Don’t overbake—cookies should be slightly soft in the center.
  • Chill dough if too soft to handle.
Microwave Apple Crisp in a Mug
Adapted from https://redrecipe.com/microwave-apple-crisp-in-a-mug/
Tags: dessert
Ingredients
  • 1 medium-sized apple (like Honeycrisp, Fuji, or Gala), peeled, cored, and diced
  • 3 tablespoons All-Purpose Flour
  • 2 tablespoons Rolled Oats
  • 2 tablespoons Brown Sugar, packed.
  • 1 T Granulated Sugar
  • Butter, Cold and Cubed: 2 tablespoons (or coconut oil for vegan option)
  • ½ t Cinnamon
  • ⅛ Nutmeg t (optional)
  • A pinch of Salt
  • 1 T Water
Steps
  1. Prepare the Apple: Peel, core, and dice your apple into small, bite-sized pieces. The smaller the pieces, the quicker they will cook in the microwave. Place the diced apple in a microwave-safe mug (about 12-16 ounces in size).
  2. Make the Apple Base: Add a sprinkle of cinnamon (about ⅛ teaspoon) and a teaspoon of brown sugar to the diced apples in the mug. Toss gently to coat the apples evenly. Add the tablespoon of water to the bottom of the mug. This will help create a bit of steam and ensure the apples cook through.
  3. Combine Dry Topping Ingredients: In a small bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rolled oats, remaining brown sugar, granulated sugar, cinnamon, nutmeg (if using), and salt. Make sure everything is well combined and there are no lumps of brown sugar.
  4. Add Cold Butter (or Coconut Oil): Add the cold, cubed butter (or coconut oil) to the dry ingredients. Using your fingertips or a pastry blender, cut the butter into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. You want to see small pieces of butter mixed throughout the flour mixture. This cold butter is crucial for creating a crisp topping in the microwave.
  5. Assemble the Crisp: Sprinkle the crumb topping evenly over the diced apples in the mug, ensuring the apples are mostly covered. Gently press the topping down slightly to help it adhere to the apples and cook evenly.
  6. Microwave: Place the mug in the microwave. Microwave on high power for 1 minute and 30 seconds to 2 minutes and 30 seconds, or until the topping is golden brown and the apples are tender when pierced with a fork. Microwave times may vary depending on your microwave’s wattage, so start with 1 minute 30 seconds and add 30-second intervals until done. Watch carefully to prevent burning.
  7. Cool Slightly and Serve: Carefully remove the mug from the microwave (it will be hot!). Let it cool for a minute or two before serving. This allows the crisp to set slightly and prevents burning your mouth. Enjoy your warm and delicious Microwave Apple Crisp in a Mug immediately!
Orange Chicken Instant Pot
Adapted from https://thecleaneatingcouple.com/instant-pot-orange-chicken/
Tags: dinner
Servings: 4 people PRINT PIN RATE
Ingredients
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breasts cut into 1 inch cubes
  • 1 cup orange juice
  • 3 tablespoons ketchup
  • 2 tablespoons coconut aminos
  • 2 tablespoons brown sugar or honey
  • 1 tablespoon minced fresh ginger minced
  • 1 tablespoon minced garlic about 4 garlic cloves
  • 1 tablespoon orange zest
  • 1 tablespoon rice vinegar
  • 1 tablespoon cornstarch
  • 2 tablespoons water
Steps
  1. Pro Tip: To get the most tender chicken, before cooking: clean chicken of any excess fat. Pound with a meat mallet to about 1 inch thickness and then cut into cubes
  2. Set the instant pot on the Sauté function. Add 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot. Add the chicken pieces and cook until the chicken browns, about 10 minutes.
  3. While the chicken cooks, mix together orange juice, ketchup, coconut aminos, brown sugar, ginger, garlic, orange zest and rice vinegar. Do not add corn starch or water.
  4. Add the sauce mixture to the pot. Stir until all of the ingredients are combined and chicken is well coated in the sauce. Make sure to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the instant pot to avoid getting a burn notice.
  5. Close the lid and set the valve to Sealing. Cook on High Pressure for 7 minutes.
  6. Once the pressure-cooking cycle is over, allow the pressure to release naturally for 10 minutes.
  7. Release any remaining pressure by opening the valve. Open the lid and press the Cancel button.
  8. Press the SAUTE button.
  9. In a bowl whisk together cornstarch and water until completely dissolved to make a cornstarch slurry.
  10. Once the sauce starts to bubble, slowly pour in the cornstarch slurry while whisking the sauce.
  11. Allow the sauce to bubble and cook for 1-2 minutes or until the sauce thickens.
  12. Once the sauce has thickened, press the cancel button.
  13. Serve over rice, cauliflower rice, steamed veggies or however you like!
    14. Stove Top Instructions:
  14. Pro Tip: To get the most tender chicken, before cooking: clean chicken of any excess fat. Pound with a meat mallet to about 1 inch thickness and then cut into cubes
  15. In a large sauté pan, add 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Add the chicken pieces and cook until the chicken changes color to light brown (about 10 minutes)
  16. While the chicken cooks, mix together orange juice, ketchup, coconut aminos, brown sugar, ginger, garlic, orange zest and rice vinegar. Do not add corn starch or water.
  17. Add the sauce mixture to the pot. Stir until all of the ingredients are combined and chicken is well coated in the sauce.
  18. Cook the chicken/sauce over medium high heat for 2-3 minutes
  19. In a bowl whisk together cornstarch and water until completely dissolved to make a cornstarch slurry.
  20. Turn the heat up on the pan. Once the sauce starts to bubble, slowly pour in the cornstarch slurry while whisking the sauce.
  21. Allow the sauce to bubble and cook for 1-2 minutes or until the sauce thickens.
Notes
  • There are many ways to customize this recipe. Look above for the green Substitutions section for a detailed list on how to swap out each ingredient!
Creamy Corn Chowder
Adapted from https://janetdishes.com/creamy-corn-chowder-recipe/
Tags: Ben dinner freezer meals soups
I made this. Is starred. Rated 5/5
Servings: 4 to 6
Ingredients
  • 2 tablespoons butter
  • 1T onion
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
  • 3 cups vegetable or chicken broth
  • 3 cups corn kernels (fresh, frozen, or canned)
  • 2 medium potatoes, diced
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
Steps
  1. Melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add chopped onion and sauté until soft and translucent, about 5 minutes.
  2. Stir in the garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.
  3. Sprinkle flour over the aromatics and stir for a minute to form a light roux.
  4. Slowly pour in the broth, stirring continuously, and add diced potatoes.
  5. Bring to a boil, then reduce to simmer for 12 to 15 minutes until potatoes are tender.
  6. Add corn and cook for another 5 to 7 minutes until heated through.
  7. Stir in heavy cream and thyme. Simmer gently without boiling.
  8. Season with salt and pepper.
  9. For a creamier texture, use an immersion blender to partially blend the soup.
  10. Serve hot with optional toppings like crispy bacon, chives, or shredded cheese.
Notes
  • Ben likes it when the soup is boiled off
90 Calorie Cherry Cheesecake Cups
Adapted from https://www.thestatenislandfamily.com/90-calorie-cherry-cheesecake-cups/
Tags: dessert healthier
Ingredients
  • 2 1/2 Cups Fage 0% Greek Yogurt
  • 1 oz package cheesecake flavor Jell-O sugar free fat free
  • 1 cup Comstock no sugar added cherry pie filling
Steps
  1. Mix yogurt with pudding mix, (if it’s too thick you can add 1/8 cup fat free milk or almond milk) put 1/2 cup into each container, top with 1/4 cup cherries.
  2. Easy and so good!
Easy Oreo Fluff Salad
Adapted from https://www.crazyforcrust.com/easy-oreo-fluff-salad/
Tags: dessert
I made this. Rated 2/5
Servings: 8
Ingredients
  • 1 3.4 ounce box instant vanilla pudding mix
  • 1 ½ cups nonfat milk
  • 8 ounces Cool Whip
  • 30 Oreos chopped
  • 2 cups mini marshmallows
Steps
  1. Whisk together pudding mix and milk. Let sit for about 3-4 minutes, or until soft set. Fold in Cool Whip.
  2. Add chopped Oreos and marshmallows. Stir to combine. Chill an our or so before serving. Cover and refrigerate for up to 4 days.
Notes
  • To make this a Weight Watchers Recipe:
  • Use Sugar-Free pudding mix and sugar-free Cool Whip
  • Either use sugar-free Oreos or use Oreo Thins
  • Omit marshmallows
  • Reduce milk amount to 1 cup
Edible Sugar Cookie Dough for One
Adapted from https://cookiesfordays.com/edible-sugar-cookie-dough-for-one/
Tags: dessert
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/4 cup + 1 tbsp all purpose flour
  • 2 tbsp salted butter softened
  • 3 tbsp granulated sugar
  • 1/2 tbsp milk
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla
  • 1 pinch salt
  • sprinkles optional
Steps
  1. Add your flour to a microwave safe bowl. Microwave for 30 seconds.
  2. In a separate bowl, use a fork to combine the butter and sugar until completely combined and fluffy.
  3. Stir in the milk and vanilla.
  4. Add the flour and a pinch of salt. Fold until well combined.
  5. Fold in sprinkles, if desired.
  6. Enjoy!
Chocolate Peppermint Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1 tbsp cocoa powder
  • 1/2 tsp peppermint extract
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
  • 1 tbsp chocolate chips (optional)
  • Crushed candy canes, for garnish (optional)
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, cocoa powder, peppermint extract, and sweetener.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, stir the oats again. Add a splash of milk if needed to reach your desired consistency.
  5. Top with chocolate chips and crushed candy canes if desired before serving.
Gingerbread Overnight Oats Recipes
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1 tbsp molasses
  • 1/2 tsp ground ginger
  • 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp ground cloves
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, molasses, ginger, cinnamon, cloves, vanilla extract, and sweetener if using.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, give the oats a good stir. If the oats are too thick, add a splash of milk.
  5. Serve cold or warm, topped with a sprinkle of cinnamon or your favorite nuts, and a gingerbread man.
Apple Pie Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup unsweetened apple juice or almond milk
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1/2 apple, diced
  • 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
  • 1/4 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey (optional)
  • Chopped nuts or granola, for topping
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, apple juice or milk, Greek yogurt, diced apple, cinnamon, nutmeg, vanilla extract, and sweetener if using.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, give the oats a good stir and add a splash of milk if necessary.
  5. Top with chopped nuts or granola and some extra chopped apple if you like, before serving.
Eggnog Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup eggnog (store-bought or homemade)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
  • 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, eggnog, Greek yogurt, vanilla extract, nutmeg, and cinnamon.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
    3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  3. In the morning, stir the oats and add more eggnog or milk if needed.
  4. Serve cold or warm, topped with a sprinkle of nutmeg.
Pumpkin Pie Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/

Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1/4 cup pumpkin puree
  • 1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp ground nutmeg
  • 1/4 tsp ground ginger
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, pumpkin puree, cinnamon, nutmeg, ginger, and maple syrup.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, stir the oats and add more milk if necessary.
  5. Serve cold or warm, topped with a sprinkle of cinnamon.
Festive Cinnamon Roll Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
  • Icing: 1 tbsp cream cheese, 1 tsp powdered sugar, and 1-2 tsp milk
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, cinnamon, vanilla extract, and maple syrup.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, prepare the icing by mixing cream cheese, powdered sugar, and milk until smooth.
  5. Stir the oats and add a splash of milk if needed.
  6. Drizzle the icing over the oats before serving.
Chocolate Cherry Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
Tags: breakfast healthier
Servings: 1
Ingredients
  • 1/2 cup rolled oats
  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1 tbsp cocoa powder
  • 1/4 cup fresh or frozen cherries, pitted and chopped
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, cocoa powder, cherries, maple syrup, and vanilla extract.
    2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  2. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  3. In the morning, stir the oats and add a splash of milk if needed.
  4. Serve cold or warm, topped with additional cherries.
Almond Joy Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/

  • 1/2 cup almond milk (or milk of choice)
  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1 tbsp cocoa powder
  • 1 tbsp shredded coconut
  • 1 tbsp sliced almonds
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, almond milk, Greek yogurt, cocoa powder, shredded coconut, almonds, maple syrup, and vanilla extract.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, stir the oats and add more milk if needed.
  5. Serve cold or warm, topped with extra coconut and almonds.

  • 1/4 cup Greek yogurt
  • 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
  • 1/4 tsp ground ginger
  • 1/4 tsp ground cardamom
  • 1 tbsp maple syrup or honey
Steps
  1. In a mason jar or bowl, combine the oats, chai tea, Greek yogurt, vanilla extract, cinnamon, ginger, cardamom, and sweetener.
  2. Stir well to combine all ingredients.
  3. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
  4. In the morning, stir the oats and add more chai tea or milk if needed.

Peppermint Mocha Overnight Oats
Adapted from https://slimmingviolet.com/christmas-overnight-oats-recipes/
